architecture_summary: SwinIR is an image restoration model based on the Swin Transformer, composed of shallow feature extraction, deep feature extraction with residual Swin Transformer blocks (RSTB), and high-quality image reconstruction. It uses local attention and shifted window mechanisms for restoration tasks including image super-resolution, image denoising, and JPEG compression artifact reduction.

novel_contributions: We propose a strong baseline model SwinIR for image restoration based on the Swin Transformer.
SwinIR consists of three parts: shallow feature extraction, deep feature extraction and high-quality image reconstruction.
The deep feature extraction module is composed of several residual Swin Transformer blocks (RSTB), each of which has several Swin Transformer layers together with a residual connection.
SwinIR outperforms state-of-the-art methods on different tasks by up to 0.14~0.45dB, while the total number of parameters can be reduced by up to 67%.

key_claims.claim: SwinIR is a strong baseline model for image restoration based on the Swin Transformer, with shallow feature extraction, deep feature extraction, and high-quality image reconstruction modules.
SwinIR outperforms state-of-the-art methods on three representative tasks by up to 0.14~0.45dB, while reducing the total number of parameters by up to 67%.
The deep feature extraction module uses residual Swin Transformer blocks (RSTB), each containing several Swin Transformer layers and a residual connection.
For classical image SR, SwinIR achieves best performance on almost all five benchmark datasets for all scale factors when trained on DIV2K.
For lightweight image SR, SwinIR outperforms competitive methods by a PSNR margin of up to 0.53dB on different benchmark datasets.
For JPEG compression artifact reduction, the proposed SwinIR has average PSNR gains of at least 0.11dB and 0.07dB on two testing datasets for different quality factors.
key_claims.evidence_section: 4.4. Results on JPEG Compression Artifact Reduction

key_claims.claim: For grayscale and color image denoising, SwinIR achieves better performance than all compared methods and surpasses DRUNet by up to 0.3dB on Urban100.
